medications.indication_rationale: Mild iron deficiency is managed electively by the GP after confirming the picture; not an acute telehealth prescription tonight.
Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting panic attack; paced breathing and reassurance are first-line.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do slow paced/box breathing now (in 4, hold 4, out 4, hold 4), log out of the pathology portal, and avoid researching the diagnosis tonight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reverses hyperventilation and terminates the panic cycle.

patient_education_points.point: What you're feeling — racing heart, chest tightness, fast breathing and tingling fingers/lips — is very typical of a panic attack, especially as it started the moment you read the results. Your home oxygen reading is normal.
The results themselves are not an emergency: early coeliac disease and mild low iron develop slowly and are sorted out with your GP, not tonight.
Call 000 or go to ED if you get crushing chest pain that spreads to your arm/jaw, sharp pain when breathing with a swollen calf, you faint, or you pass black tarry stools or vomit blood.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Coeliac confirmation/gastroenterology referral, iron studies and replacement, and anxiety support if panic attacks recur.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Exertional/radiating chest pain, pleuritic pain with leg swelling, syncope, or GI bleeding — ED/000.

must_include_items: Screen ACS, PE and GI-bleed/severe-anaemia red flags before attributing to panic
Confidently de-escalate a low-risk panic attack with paced breathing and reassurance
acceptable_alternatives: Arranging routine GP follow-up for coeliac and iron
errors_of_omission: No cardiac/PE screen before the panic label
errors_of_commission: Reflex ambulance/ED dispatch for a clearly low-risk panic attack
Telling her the coeliac/iron results are an emergency
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case with a bidirectional trap: passing requires BOTH a red-flag screen (so ACS/PE aren't missed) AND confident non-escalation of the low-risk panic attack. Failing either direction fails the case.
